data.title: Pi Network Faces Centralization Concerns, User Frustration
data.description:
The pi Network, a cryptocurrency project that has garnered significant attention, is facing growing concerns over its centralization issues. The core team of the Pi Network retains control over approximately 82.8 billion Pi Coins, which constitutes over 82% of the total 100 billion supply. This concentrated ownership has raised alarms within the cryptocurrency community regarding governance transparency and decentralization.

A healthy blockchain network typically requires a diverse ownership spread to mitigate risks associated with a single point of failure and to promote trust among its users. The substantial control held by the core team creates discomfort, as it deviates from the principles of decentralization that are fundamental to blockchain technology.

In addition to centralization concerns, the Pi Network's infrastructure is also under scrutiny. Currently, the network operates with only 43 nodes and three validators, a stark contrast to established networks like Bitcoin and Ethereum, which have over 21,000 and 6,600 nodes respectively. This limited number of nodes and validators raises questions about the network's capacity for decentralized verification, increasing its vulnerability to manipulation and central authority.

The shortage of nodes reflects a developmental stage that demands attention. Expanding the number of validators is crucial for enhancing network security and trustworthiness, two core principles in the cryptocurrency realm. The minimalization of nodes and validators raises severe questions about the network's decentralization and overall integrity.

Recent privacy updates by the Pi Network have introduced the use of ChatGPT for its Know Your Customer (KYC) procedures. While this move aims to streamline user identification and enhance security, it also raises complex issues around user data privacy and security. The involvement of AI in handling sensitive data could lead to public apprehension and requires transparency to avoid potential misuse.

The overall sentiment surrounding the Pi Network has noticeably soured. Many users have expressed frustration over the mainnet’s challenging transition, particularly the lengthy tokens lockup period, which makes accessing their coins cumbersome. Consequently, the community’s disenchantment has even forced some users to attempt selling their accounts, further signaling a decline in confidence.

The issues surrounding the Pi Network, ranging from its centralized control of Pi Coin to infrastructure weaknesses and evolving privacy strategies, culminate in a complex landscape that users must navigate cautiously. As the Pi Network seeks to establish itself in the competitive crypto space, addressing these concerns transparently and effectively will be crucial for revitalizing user trust. The future viability of the network largely hinges on solving these pressing issues while maintaining an open line of communication with its user base.

data.title: Europe Fights Back: Retaliation Against Trump's 25% Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! The trade war is heating up, and Europe is not going down without a fight. President Trump's latest move to impose a 25% tariff on steel and a 10% tariff on aluminum imports from the EU has sparked a firestorm of retaliation. EC President von der Leyen has promised "firm and proportionate countermeasures," and you better believe she means business. This is not just a skirmish; it's a full-blown trade war, and the stakes are higher than ever.



The immediate impact on the European Union is going to be brutal. Eurofer estimates potential losses of up to 3.7 million metric tonnes of steel exports to the US, representing 16% of total EU steel exports as of 2024. That's a massive hit to their economy, and it's going to depress European steel prices further. Mills are already backing away from US transactions due to tariff uncertainty and fears of retroactive application. The market is going to be volatile, with mills and traders taking defensive positions to hedge against potential losses.

But Europe isn't going to take this lying down. They're fighting back with targeted tariffs on US goods, likely focusing on politically sensitive exports. This could lead to a potential WTO challenge and possible coordination with other affected nations, such as Canada, to magnify the impact of the objection. The EU's response is going to be significant, and it's going to disrupt global trade flows.

In the US, the supply gap of approximately 11.6 million metric tons (with US crude steel production at 81.4 million metric tons versus consumption of 93 million metric tons in 2023) will likely result in significantly higher domestic steel prices as imports become more expensive and supply tightens. US mills may increase production, but capacity constraints suggest they cannot fully close the supply gap, leading to higher manufacturing costs for downstream industries using steel as input, potentially affecting their competitiveness.

The medium-term production and price estimates suggest that the EU may experience a 5-10% reduction in production volumes in the near term as mills adjust to lost US market access. In the US, domestic production is anticipated to increase by 3-5%, constrained by capacity limits. Other markets, including Ukraine, are likely to see further production falls, amounting to several percentage points. Steel prices in the EU are expected to fall due to redirected supply staying in domestic markets and greater price pressure from foreign suppliers. The initial price fall during 2025 could amount to $15-$20 per tonne or more if appropriate safeguard measures are not introduced. In the US, steel price increases are expected to be around 2-3% as the year 2025 unfolds and beyond. Other markets are likely to experience price decreases of several percentage points as redirected supply increases price competition.

The long-term economic and political consequences of these escalating trade tensions are going to be severe. The proposed tariffs by President Trump on imports from the EU, set to take effect from March 12, 2025, will impose a 25% duty on steel imports and 10% on aluminum imports. This represents a significant shift from the previous policy that had exempted the EU through tariff rate quotas established in December 2021. The immediate impact on the European Union is likely to be substantial, with Eurofer estimating potential losses of up to 3.7 million metric tonnes of steel exports to the US, representing 16% of total EU steel exports as of 2024. This will likely depress European steel prices further and delay any price recovery, leading to a 5-10% reduction in EU production volumes in the near-term as mills adjust to lost US market access.

In the US, the supply gap of approximately 11.6 million metric tons (with US crude steel production at 81.4 million metric tons versus consumption of 93 million metric tons in 2023) will likely result in significantly higher domestic steel prices as imports become more expensive and supply tightens. US mills may increase production, but capacity constraints suggest they cannot fully close the supply gap, leading to higher manufacturing costs for downstream industries using steel as input, potentially affecting their competitiveness.

Politically, the EU's response is likely to be significant, with EC President von der Leyen promising "firm and proportionate countermeasures." Historical precedent suggests targeted tariffs on US goods, likely focusing on politically sensitive US exports. This could lead to a potential WTO challenge and possible coordination with other affected nations, such as Canada, to magnify the impact of the objection.

These tensions could influence future trade policies and agreements by leading to increased protectionism and retaliatory measures, further disrupting global supply chains and increasing uncertainty for businesses. The economic and political fallout could also strain diplomatic relations between the US and the EU, making it more challenging to reach consensus on other international issues. Additionally, the escalating trade tensions could prompt other countries to reassess their trade relationships with the US and the EU, potentially leading to a realignment of global trade flows and alliances.

So, what does this mean for you, the investor? It means you need to be prepared for volatility. The market hates uncertainty, and this trade war is the definition of uncertainty. But it also means opportunity. Those who can navigate these choppy waters will be rewarded handsomely. So, stay tuned, stay informed, and most importantly, stay nimble. This is a no-brainer!

data.title: Tariff Turmoil: U.S. and Canada Clash, Markets Reel
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! The trade war between the U.S. and Canada is heating up, and the markets are feeling the heat. President Trump has just signed executive orders to impose tariffs on Canada, Mexico, and China, and the fallout is already being felt. The 10 percent tariffs on all imports from China took effect on February 4, 2025, and the tariffs on Canada and Mexico are set to take effect on March 4, 2025. But that's not all—Trump has also announced plans to increase the tariffs on China by another 10 percent beginning March 4. This is a game-changer, folks, and you need to be prepared.



The proposed tariffs on Canada and Mexico are estimated to reduce long-run GDP by 0.3 percent, which is a significant hit to the economy. This reduction in GDP can lead to decreased consumer spending and investment, which in turn can negatively affect the stock prices of companies in various sectors. For example, sectors such as automotive, manufacturing, and agriculture, which rely heavily on cross-border trade, are likely to be most vulnerable to these fluctuations. Companies in these sectors may face increased costs due to tariffs, which can reduce their profitability and, consequently, their stock prices.

But it's not just the U.S. that's feeling the pain. China has already announced retaliation on about $13.9 billion worth of U.S. exports at rates of 10 percent and 15 percent, which took effect on February 10. This is a classic case of tit-for-tat, and it's only going to get worse. The market hates uncertainty, and this trade war is creating a lot of it. So, what should you do?

First, stay away from sectors that are heavily reliant on cross-border trade with Canada, such as automotive, aerospace, and agriculture. These sectors are likely to be hit the hardest by the tariffs, and their stock prices are likely to take a beating. Second, consider investing in domestic production, as businesses seek to avoid the increased costs of imported goods. However, be aware that this could also lead to increased competition and lower profit margins for domestic producers, as they may be forced to compete with lower-cost imports from other countries.

But don't just take my word for it. Look at the historical data. The 2018-2019 trade war tariffs imposed by Trump and retained by Biden reduced long-run GDP by 0.2 percent, the capital stock by 0.1 percent, and employment by 142,000 full-time equivalent jobs. This is a clear indication that tariffs have a negative impact on the economy, and the current tariffs are likely to have a similar effect.

So, what's the bottom line? The ongoing tariff war between the U.S. and Canada is a major concern for investors, and it's important to be prepared for the potential long-term economic implications. The tariffs are likely to increase prices for consumers and businesses, which can lead to a decrease in consumer spending and business investment, further exacerbating the economic slowdown. Additionally, the tariffs could lead to a reduction in employment, as businesses may be forced to cut jobs in response to increased costs and decreased demand.

But don't let this scare you. There are still opportunities out there, and you need to be ready to seize them. So, stay informed, stay vigilant, and stay ahead of the game. The market is a fickle beast, but with the right strategy, you can tame it. BOO-YAH!

data.title: Daily Corporate Dividends on Mar 12 2025
data.description:
Day traders leveraging the dividend capture strategy today have a variety of stocks to choose from. Stocks like gdev are paying a significant USD 3.31 per share, while others, such as DSX, are offering lower dividends at USD 0.01 per share. As always, investors must weigh the potential profit from the dividend against the risk of a falling share price.

The dividend capture strategy is a popular income-focused trading strategy among day traders. Unlike traditional approaches, this strategy involves frequent buying and selling of shares to capture the dividend payment. The appeal of this strategy lies in its simplicity, as it does not require complex fundamental analysis or charting. Investors can purchase the stock before the ex-dividend date, sell it on or after that date, and still receive the dividend payment. However, if the stock falls more than the dividend paid, the net profit will be reduced. In such cases, it may be wise to wait for the stock to rebound to its purchase price before selling, although there is also a risk that the stock may continue to decline.

data.title: Rotork plc's Stock: Momentum or Mirage?
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! We're diving headfirst into the world of Rotork plc (LON:ROR), a company that's been making waves with its recent financial performance. The stock has seen some serious momentum, and you need to know if this is a call for deeper study or just another flash in the pan. Let's break it down!



First things first, let's talk about the numbers. Rotork plc has been on a tear, with revenue growth of 4.9% year-on-year and an 8.2% increase on an organic constant currency (OCC) basis. That's not just growth; that's *Growth, growth, growth!* The company's adjusted operating margin improved by 70 basis points to 23.6%, which is a massive win for investors. This isn't just a one-off; Rotork has been delivering consistent revenue growth and margin expansion for years.

Now, let's talk about the strategic initiatives. Rotork has been busy making moves. They've agreed to acquire Noah, a leading South Korean electric actuator manufacturer, for an enterprise value of £44 million. This acquisition is a game-changer, broadening and strengthening Rotork's electric actuator offering. And if that wasn't enough, they've announced another £50 million share buyback. That's right, folks! They're returning cash to shareholders and reducing the number of shares outstanding. This is a no-brainer for investors looking for a stable income stream and strong financial performance.

But wait, there's more! Rotork's financial performance is nothing short of stellar. They reported revenue of £754.43 million and earnings of £103.59 million in the last 12 months. The adjusted operating margin was 23.6%, which is a significant improvement from the previous year. And let's not forget about the strong cash flow and net cash position of £125.33 million. This provides financial flexibility for further investments and shareholder returns. The company's return on equity (ROE) of 17.17% and return on invested capital (ROIC) of 13.51% indicate efficient use of capital and strong profitability.

Now, let's talk about valuation. Rotork plc's current valuation metrics suggest that the company is attractively priced relative to its peers and historical averages. The trailing PE ratio of 27.65 suggests that investors are willing to pay a premium for Rotork's earnings, which could indicate expectations of strong future growth. However, the forward PE ratio of 19.47 is lower, suggesting that future earnings are expected to grow at a faster rate than current earnings, making the stock more attractive on a forward-looking basis. The EV/EBITDA ratio of 17.71 is also a key metric for valuation. This ratio indicates how much investors are willing to pay for each pound of earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. A lower EV/EBITDA ratio generally suggests that the company is undervalued relative to its peers, while a higher ratio suggests it is overvalued. In Rotork's case, this ratio is within a reasonable range, indicating that the company's valuation is in line with industry standards.

So, what's the bottom line? Rotork plc's recent financial performance, strategic initiatives, and strong cash flow position make it an attractive investment opportunity. The company's consistent revenue growth, margin expansion, and efficient use of capital indicate that Rotork has significant growth potential. If you're looking for a stock with strong momentum and a solid foundation for future growth, Rotork plc is a no-brainer. Don't miss out on this opportunity to get in on the ground floor of a company that's poised for continued success. BOO-YAH!

data.title: Nvidia's GTC 2025: Unveiling AI Innovations and Pioneering a Cooling Revolution
data.description:
Nvidia is set to host the highly-anticipated GTC 2025 conference from March 17 to 21 in San Jose, California. The event is expected to attract 25,000 in-person participants and around 300,000 online attendees, making it a key event in the AI industry calendar. This year's conference will showcase Nvidia's advanced AI and accelerated computing platforms and their applications in diverse fields such as climate research, healthcare, cybersecurity, humanoid robotics, and autonomous driving.

A major highlight of the conference is the keynote speech by nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on March 19. He is expected to introduce the GB300 and B300 series chips, building on last year's launch of the Blackwell AI platform. Despite initial production challenges, Blackwell has been successfully mass-produced, generating substantial sales, and the new chip series is anticipated to bring significant performance enhancements. The B300, specifically, is expected to offer a 50% improvement over its predecessor, the B200.

The introduction of water cooling technology for these new chips marks a significant shift from traditional air cooling methods, aligning with increased power consumption and cooling needs. This advancement is hailed as a "second cooling revolution," with Nvidia set to quadruple the use of high-density water cooling pipelines compared to the previous GB200 model.

Beyond product unveilings, GTC 2025 will feature the first "Quantum Day," focusing on quantum computing's current landscape and future potential. Industry leaders such as D-Wave and IonQ will join discussions, highlighting Nvidia’s recalibrated stance on the quantum computing frontier.

In parallel, the conference will host China AI Day, a special session spotlighting advancements by leading Chinese tech firms like ByteDance and Baidu in AI models and software-hardware optimization. The event highlights China's growing influence in AI technology amidst a global AI boom.

Overall, the GTC 2025 is poised to reinforce Nvidia's leadership in AI and potentially revitalize its stock market performance. Analysts suggest the conference could catalyze market reassessment of Nvidia’s valuation, with a focus on long-term growth. Thus, GTC 2025 stands as a critical junction for Nvidia’s future trajectory in AI innovation and market positioning.

data.title: Google Eyes the Future: Strategic Acquisition of AdHawk to Boost Smart Glasses Ambitions
data.description:
Google (GOOGL.US) is reportedly in the final stages of negotiations to acquire AdHawk Microsystems, a Canadian startup renowned for its eye-tracking technology, marking another strategic move into the smart glasses market. The tech giant plans to invest $115 million in the acquisition, including a conditional $15 million payout dependent on AdHawk achieving specific performance metrics. This agreement is expected to be finalized within the week, although the deal could still face hurdles before being inked.

Founded in 2017, AdHawk has focused on developing chipsets, hardware, and software for sophisticated eye tracking. Its low power consumption components are particularly appealing, potentially outperforming current competitors by analyzing users' cornea and pupil more swiftly. The startup has attracted significant investment from tech heavyweights such as Samsung Electronics, intel, HP, and Sony, in addition to having partners like EssilorLuxottica SA in the wearable tech sector.

Eye-tracking technology is a vital element in the advancement of AR and VR headsets, arenas where Google's previous efforts were mixed. Known as a pioneer in smart glasses technology with its “Glass” product launched over a decade ago, google discontinued this line two years ago due to lackluster sales. Since then, it has been exploring opportunities to re-enter the market, propelled by the potential of artificial intelligence, alongside partnerships with Samsung, and years of testing new smart glass prototypes. AdHawk’s cutting-edge technology might allow Google to accelerate its smart glasses launch.

Earlier this year, Google reinforced its commitment to the headset and smart glasses market with a $250 million acquisition of a portion of HTC's XR division, enhancing its pool of engineers and technology assets related to wearables. By integrating AdHawk's promising eye-tracking capabilities, Google is set to advance in the smart glasses domain, possibly reshaping how individuals interact with technology in personal and professional spaces.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Takes $138M Position With 50x Leverage, Eyes $993K Profit
data.description:
A significant development has occurred in the cryptocurrency market as an Ethereum (ETH) whale has taken a substantial position worth $138 million with a leverage of 50 times. This strategic move is aimed at capitalizing on potential price fluctuations, with the whale targeting profits of approximately $993,000. The whale's decision to increase its ETH holdings by 10,003 units underscores a bullish sentiment towards the cryptocurrency, despite the inherent risks associated with high leverage trading.

The whale's aggressive stance reflects a confidence in the future performance of ETH, which has been a subject of interest for many institutional investors. The use of 50x leverage amplifies both the potential gains and the risks involved. While the whale stands to gain significantly if the market moves in their favor, a downturn could result in substantial losses. This move highlights the high-stakes nature of cryptocurrency trading, where large positions and high leverage can lead to either substantial profits or severe financial setbacks.

The whale's strategy of increasing ETH holdings by 10,003 units suggests a long-term bullish outlook on the cryptocurrency. This move is not just about short-term gains but also about positioning for future growth. The whale's decision to leverage their position to such an extent indicates a strong belief in the potential of ETH to appreciate in value. This confidence is likely based on a combination of technical analysis, market trends, and broader economic factors that influence the cryptocurrency market.

The potential profit of $993,000 from this position underscores the lucrative nature of cryptocurrency trading for those with the capital and risk tolerance to engage in high-leverage strategies. However, it also serves as a reminder of the volatility and unpredictability of the market. The whale's move is a testament to the high-risk, high-reward nature of cryptocurrency trading, where large positions and high leverage can lead to either substantial profits or severe financial setbacks.

In summary, the ETH whale's decision to take a $138 million position with 50x leverage and eyeing $993,000 in profits reflects a bullish sentiment towards the cryptocurrency. This move underscores the high-stakes nature of cryptocurrency trading, where large positions and high leverage can lead to either substantial profits or severe financial setbacks. The whale's strategy of increasing ETH holdings by 10,003 units suggests a long-term bullish outlook on the cryptocurrency, based on a combination of technical analysis, market trends, and broader economic factors.

data.title: Crypto Investors Bypass Locked Tokens, Sparking Manipulation Fears
data.description:
Cryptocurrency investors have been discovered engaging in intricate arrangements with market makers to circumvent locked token agreements, enabling them to profit from their digital assets despite imposed restrictions. These arrangements typically involve investors partnering with market makers who facilitate the sale of tokens on their behalf, thereby bypassing the lock-up periods mandated by initial coin offerings (ICOs) or other token distribution methods. Such practices often include off-exchange trades and other complex financial strategies that allow investors to liquidate their holdings without causing market disruptions that could alert regulators or other market participants.

The practice of bypassing locked token arrangements has sparked concerns about market manipulation and the integrity of token distribution processes. By enabling investors to sell tokens before the lock-up period ends, these arrangements can skew market prices and compromise the fairness of token sales. Regulators and industry participants are closely observing these activities, as they could have broader implications for the stability and transparency of the cryptocurrency market.

The legal and regulatory environment surrounding cryptocurrencies is rapidly evolving, with authorities increasingly examining market practices to ensure compliance with existing laws. The use of market makers to facilitate the sale of locked tokens underscores the need for clearer guidelines and enforcement mechanisms to prevent abuse. As the cryptocurrency market continues to expand, it is crucial for regulators to proactively address these issues to maintain investor confidence and promote a fair and transparent trading environment.

Recent developments in the cryptocurrency market highlight the importance of robust regulatory frameworks and industry self-governance. As investors and market makers continue to explore new ways to monetize their digital assets, it is essential for all stakeholders to collaborate to ensure that these activities are conducted in a manner that is fair, transparent, and compliant with applicable laws. By doing so, the cryptocurrency market can continue to thrive and attract new participants while also protecting the interests of existing investors and maintaining market integrity.

data.title: Rheinmetall's 36% Sales Surge: A Military Order Boom on the Horizon
data.description:
Düsseldorf-based Rheinmetall AG has closed the third quarter of 2024 with record-breaking sales and earnings, driven by an unprecedented surge in demand for military equipment. The company's sales increased by 36% to €6,268 million, marking a significant milestone in its financial performance. This growth is attributed to the dynamic market situation in the military sector, with high demand from the armed forces of Germany, EU, and NATO nations, as well as ongoing aid for Ukraine. The civilian sector, however, remained slightly below the previous year's performance.

The company's operating result amounted to €705 million, a 72% increase compared to the previous year. This improvement was driven by sales growth and the earnings contribution from Rheinmetall Expal Munitions, which was acquired in August 2023. The operating margin improved to 11.3%, up from 8.9% in the previous year. Earnings per share from continuing operations also improved, rising from €4.88 to €7.32 in the first nine months of 2024.

Rheinmetall's operating free cash flow advanced significantly, increasing by €527 million to €99 million. This positive development was driven by large-volume customer advance payments, although it was mitigated by multiple capex projects and the build-up of inventories for the anticipated sales development in the fourth quarter.

The value of Rheinmetall Nomination, which includes traditional incoming orders and the volume from future call-offs under new framework agreements, increased by 48% to €21.4 billion. This was driven by orders from Germany, primarily from the special fund for the Bundeswehr, and orders to aid Ukraine. As a result, the Rheinmetall Backlog increased by 41% to €51.9 billion, indicating a strong order pipeline.

The company's Vehicle Systems division, which focuses on military wheeled and tracked vehicles, saw a 52% increase in sales to €2,537 million. This growth was driven by the delivery of pre-produced swap body systems and the start of tactical vehicle programs. The division's backlog rose by 37% to €6.8 billion, with the largest orders including a new framework agreement to supply UTF category military trucks to the Bundeswehr worth €2.9 billion and an order for the manufacture and supply of the German armed forces’ heavy weapon carrier based on the Boxer wheeled vehicle worth over €1.6 billion.

Rheinmetall's strategic plan, which includes capacity expansion, acquisitions, and global partnerships, positions the company to capitalize on the current and future demand for military equipment. The company has "massively expanded capacities" and is building new plants in countries like Lithuania, Hungary, Romania, and Ukraine. These expansions ensure that Rheinmetall can meet the increased demand for military equipment from Germany, EU, NATO nations, and Ukraine.

The company's acquisitions, such as Rheinmetall Expal Munitions, have significantly contributed to its growth. The acquisition generated €230 million in sales during the first half of 2024 and has been a key driver in the substantial growth of the Weapon and Ammunition segment. The acquisition has also helped in increasing the operating result margin to 19.5% despite rising labor and material costs.

Rheinmetall has secured numerous framework agreements and orders, which provide a steady stream of revenue and ensure future growth. For example, the company has a new framework agreement to supply UTF category military trucks to the Bundeswehr worth €2.9 billion, and an order for the manufacture and supply of the German armed forces’ heavy weapon carrier based on the Boxer wheeled vehicle worth over €1.6 billion. These orders, along with the associated service contracts, ensure that Rheinmetall will continue to see growth in sales in the years to come.

The company's global partnerships bring it closer to its goal of becoming a global champion in the defense industry. For instance, the company's largest contracts to date include a €1.6 billion order to produce and deliver heavy weapon carriers based on the Boxer wheeled vehicle for the German armed forces, and a service contract for these vehicles worth over €620 million.



In conclusion, Rheinmetall's strategic plan, which includes capacity expansion, acquisitions, framework agreements, global partnerships, and a strong order pipeline, positions the company to capitalize on the current and future demand for military equipment. The company's proactive approach and strategic investments have enabled it to achieve record highs in sales and earnings, and it is well-positioned to continue this growth in the coming years. The anticipated 'major' military order boom is expected to drive further growth in Rheinmetall's sales and earnings, with the company aiming for an operating result margin at the upper end of the guidance.

data.title: U.S. stocks are at risk of a second wave of sell-off, and Tiger Brokers is bullish on opportunities in Chinese tech stocks.
data.description: Tiger Brokers (Hong Kong) Asset Management's Chief Investment Officer, Chen Qingwei, believes that the first phase of the decline in US stocks has been completed, and the valuations of many large technology stocks have temporarily returned to reasonable levels. However, the macro risks in the US are still large. The firm believes that the technological progress of AI is accelerating this year, and more AI agents will appear in the future, which will change the existing business models and people's lifestyles, bringing about larger and more lasting profit growth. Considering the pressure faced by US stocks, the firm is optimistic about the continued upward opportunities of Chinese tech stocks this year. It is advisable to allocate valuable high dividend stocks to diversify risks.At present, US stocks have retraced all the gains since Trump's inauguration, and the S&P 500 index has reached a key support level of around 5,600 points.However, the Fed will not reduce interest rates too quickly amid uncertainties over inflation pressure from tariffs, and companies may scale back future capital expenditures amid uncertainties in the macro environment, affecting profit expectations and eventually leading to a second wave of decline, with the market sentiment deteriorating and leading to a wave of panic selling, which may push the S&P 500 back to around 4,800 points in 2021.However, Chen Qingwei said that even if the US stock market enters a bear market, a significant rebound has never been absent, and the time and extent depend on when and how much the Trump administration's tax cuts and deregulation policies will benefit the economy.The main concern for investors is the uncertainty of Trump's policies, and the technology stocks have already recorded significant gains in the past two years, so there will be more stop-loss orders. From a funding perspective, the funds that have been added to US stocks since Trump's inauguration have been squeezed in large areas, and will also bring continuous selling pressure to the possible rebound.In the long run, Tiger Brokers (Hong Kong) believes that the technological progress of AI is accelerating this year, and it is expected to bring larger and more lasting profit growth. For some leading companies with strong technological barriers in AI hardware, infrastructure and applications, their future profit growth is relatively certain, and their current valuations have obvious performance support. It is advisable to start deploying and allocating them in batches, and if the market sentiment deteriorates further due to macro factors, it will be a better opportunity.

data.title: 36Kr Holdings Inc. Earnings Call: A Deep Dive into 2024's Challenges and Future Prospects
data.description:
The earnings call for 36kr holdings inc. (NASDAQ: KRKR) on March 11, 2025, provided a comprehensive look into the company's financial performance for the second half of 2024 and the full fiscal year. The call highlighted both the challenges faced by the company and its strategic initiatives aimed at driving future growth. Let's break down the key takeaways and analyze the implications for investors.

Revenue Decline Across All Segments

The most striking revelation from the earnings call was the significant decline in total revenues. For the full year 2024, total revenues plummeted by 32% year-over-year to RMB231.1 million (US$31.7 million). This decline was evident across all business segments:
- Online advertising services revenue decreased by 24.3% to RMB180.6 million.
- Enterprise value-added services revenue plummeted by 51.3% to RMB32.8 million.
- Subscription services revenue fell by 48.5% to RMB17.6 million.

These declines suggest that 36kr is facing significant headwinds in its core revenue streams. The company's CEO, Dagang Feng, acknowledged the challenging external environment of 2024, stating, "Amid the challenging external environment of 2024, we maintained our focus on long-term strategy and achieved significant operational efficiency improvements." This indicates that the company is aware of the market challenges and is taking steps to mitigate their impact.

Cost-Cutting Measures and Operational Efficiency

One of the bright spots in the earnings call was the significant reduction in operating expenses. The company achieved a 50% year-over-year reduction in operating expenses for the second half of 2024 and a 31% reduction for the full year. This aggressive cost management has led to a decrease in general and administrative expenses from RMB72.2 million in the second half of 2023 to RMB30.3 million in the second half of 2024, representing a 58% decrease.

However, the sustainability of these cost-cutting measures and the potential risks or trade-offs need to be carefully considered. While cost-cutting measures can improve short-term financial performance, they may also limit the company's ability to innovate and maintain its competitive edge. As Mr. Dagang Feng mentioned, "Amid the challenging external environment of 2024, we maintained our focus on long-term strategy and achieved significant operational efficiency improvements." While cost-cutting measures can improve short-term financial performance, they may also limit the company's ability to innovate and maintain its competitive edge.

AI-Driven Opportunities and Innovative Content Initiatives

The company's focus on AI-driven opportunities and innovative content initiatives is a key part of its long-term growth strategy. 36Kr has been proactive in covering AI developments and launched AI-powered products like AI Text to Image and AI Media Coverage, which are designed to improve the quality and reach of its content offerings.

The company's long-term strategy also includes expanding its content ecosystem and refining its omni-channel distribution matrix. This is reflected in the 10% year-over-year increase in followers to 36 million as of the end of 2024, marking the 16th consecutive quarter of follower growth. This growth in followers indicates that the company's content initiatives are resonating with its audience and driving engagement.

Financial Performance and Outlook

Despite the cost-cutting measures, the company's financial performance remains troubling. The net loss for 2024 increased by 57.8% to RMB140.8 million, compared to RMB89.2 million in 2023. This widening net loss, coupled with the significant revenue decline, suggests that 36Kr is struggling to maintain its market position in the face of industry challenges.

Looking ahead, the company's strategic direction appears to be focused on long-term growth. The CEO mentioned, "Looking ahead to 2025, we will continue to implement our innovative content initiatives and harness AI-driven opportunities to drive the Company’s high-quality, sustainable development." However, immediate revenue recovery remains a critical challenge.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the 36Kr Holdings Inc. earnings call for the full year 2024 highlighted both the challenges and opportunities facing the company. While the significant revenue decline and widening net loss are concerning, the company's focus on AI-driven opportunities and innovative content initiatives offers a glimmer of hope for future growth. Investors should monitor key metrics such as follower growth, revenue from online advertising services, and operational efficiency to assess the success of these initiatives. The company's ability to balance short-term cost savings with long-term strategic investments will be crucial for its sustainable growth and competitiveness.

data.title: TSMC Eyes Intel Foundry Takeover, Courts Nvidia, AMD, and Broadcom for Joint Venture
data.description:
TSMC has approached U.S. chip giants nvidia, broadcom and amd about investing in a joint venture to operate Intel's foundry division, according to four sources familiar with the discussions. Qualcomm was also pitched as a potential partner, according to Reuters, citing two separate sources. 

Under the proposal, tsmc would manage Intel's foundry operations but would not take a majority stake, ensuring it holds no more than 50%, sources said. The discussions, still in early stages, align with the Trump administration's push for TSMC to play a role in reviving intel, a long-time U.S. semiconductor leader that has struggled in recent years.

Once a dominant force in the semiconductor industry, Intel has faced increasing pressure as AMD gained market share in CPUs while the company continued to burn cash in its chipmaking business. In 2024, Intel posted a staggering net loss of $18.8 billion, while its foundry division reported a book value of $108 billion in property and plant equipment.

Despite its financial troubles, Intel has resisted efforts to break apart its operations. While multiple companies have explored acquiring parts of the company, Intel has rejected discussions about selling off its chip design unit separately from the foundry business, according to sources.

However, a potential joint venture with TSMC remains on the table, with key Intel board members backing a deal. Internally, though, some executives remain firmly opposed, complicating negotiations.

Before announcing a massive $100 billion investment in U.S. semiconductor manufacturing—adding three new fabs, two advanced packaging facilities, and an R&D center—TSMC had already pitched the joint venture idea to potential partners, sources said. This investment may serve as a strategic move to preempt possible tariffs from the Trump administration on semiconductor imports.

Discussions over Intel's foundry unit have continued, with TSMC looking to secure backing from multiple chip designers. A final agreement would require approval from the Trump administration, which is keen to prevent full foreign ownership of Intel's foundry business.

Despite ongoing talks, a deal between TSMC and Intel would present significant hurdles. The two companies employ vastly different manufacturing processes, chemical compositions, and chip fabrication tools, making integration complex and costly.

Intel's most advanced production technology, known as 18A, has been a sticking point in negotiations. Two sources revealed that during talks in February, Intel executives claimed that their 18A node outperformed TSMC's 2-nanometer process.

Meanwhile, Nvidia and Broadcom are already testing Intel's 18A capabilities, with AMD also evaluating whether the process meets its needs, according to a Reuters report.

While Qualcomm previously considered acquiring all or part of Intel, sources said the company has since withdrawn from discussions.

data.title: CPI Preview: Key Data to Guide Fed's Next Move
data.description:
At 8:30 PM tonight, the U.S. February CPI report will be released. With the Federal Reserve's policy meeting next week, this CPI report is the last major data release before the meeting and will significantly impact the Fed's decision-making.

Analyst expectations for the February U.S. CPI increase of 2.9% YoY, core CPI rising 3.2%, and both headline and core monthly inflation advancing by 0.3%. These figures indicate a slight cooling compared to January's data.

Currently, a rate hold in March is almost certain (with a 95% probability), but uncertainty remains regarding the timing of the first rate cut (May or June?) and the total number of cuts this year (two or three?). Markets await CPI guidance.

Market Impact

If CPI meets expectations, market reactions will likely be muted. 

However, a sharp increase in CPI would indicate that Trump's tariff policies are significantly pushing inflation higher, leading to a steep decline in U.S. stocks and a potential surge in gold prices. 

Conversely, a sharp drop in CPI would suggest that inflation fears have been overstated, boosting expectations for a Fed rate cut in May and easing recession concerns, which could trigger a rally in U.S. stocks.

Food Inflation

Due to a widespread avian flu outbreak in the U.S., millions of hens have been culled, driving egg prices to record highs. Some economists expect February food CPI to remain on an upward trend. morgan stanley economists, led by Diego Anzoategui, stated:Wholesale prices for eggs and other food items continued rising in February, and we expect food inflation to stay above pre-pandemic trends at least through the summer.

Housing Inflation

Goldman sachs expects rents to rise by 0.27% in February (compared to +0.35% in January), with owners' equivalent rent increasing by 0.29% (vs. +0.31% in January). Additionally, Smith Travel Research reported that U.S. hotel prices declined in February, projecting a year-over-year decrease of 0.5% (compared to a 1.4% increase in January).

Transportation Costs

Goldman Sachs forecasts that used car prices will rise by 0.6%, driven by higher auction prices, but this marks a significant slowdown from January's 2.2% increase. New car prices are expected to rise by 0.3% as dealers reduced promotional discounts in February.

Auto insurance costs, driven by higher car prices, repair costs, and medical and litigation expenses, have been steadily increasing. goldman sachs estimates a 1.0% rise in February auto insurance prices.

Airfares are also expected to climb, with Goldman Sachs' analysis of ticket prices suggesting a 2.5% year-over-year increase in February.

Future Inflation Trends

Goldman Sachs analysts conclude that while Trump's tariffs could slow inflation's downward trajectory, factors such as the rebalancing of the auto market, adjustments in the rental sector, and changes in the labor market will drive inflation lower over the next year.

Other Key Factors to Watch

Some economists believe the February CPI report may show early signs of tariff impacts, particularly from the additional 10% U.S. tariffs on Chinese imports imposed in early February. Bank of America economists Stephen Juneau and Jeseo Park highlight that China accounts for a significant share of U.S. furniture, clothing, and electronics imports. If these effects are not evident in February's inflation data, they will likely emerge in the coming months.

It's also worth noting that ISM Manufacturing Prices Paid surged from 54.9 to 62.4, while ISM Services Prices rose from 60.4 to 62.6. Survey respondents noted that "the upcoming tariffs are driving up our product prices." The Federal Reserve's recent Beige Book also reported moderate price increases across regions, with some areas experiencing accelerated inflation. Businesses anticipate that potential tariffs will push prices higher, and some companies have preemptively raised prices due to tariff uncertainty and rising costs.

However, Treasury Secretary Vicente dismissed concerns that tariffs would cause persistent inflation. He argued that the Fed should treat tariffs as a "one-time factor" and that their inflationary impact will be temporary, without long-term spillover effects.

data.title: BHB Latest Report
data.description:
Financial Performance

Bar Harbor (stock code: BHB) recorded a total operating revenue of RMB384.59 million as of December 31, 2024, up 3.89% from RMB374.89 million as of December 31, 2023. This growth indicates a slight improvement in the company's revenue generation, possibly reflecting an enhanced sales capacity in the market.

Key Financial Data

1. Increased Demand: The growth in total operating revenue is related to increased market demand, particularly in insurance business revenue.

2. Revenue Improvement: Revenue increased from RMB125.89 million to RMB135.50 million, up 7.67%, indicating growth in the sales of the company's core business.

3. Cost Control Pressure: Although total operating revenue has grown, earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) decreased from RMB571.17 million to RMB526.14 million, indicating challenges in controlling costs and expenses.

4. Tax Impact on Net Profit: Changes in tax expenses support net profit, with tax expenses of RMB25.51 million in 2024, slightly lower than RMB26.44 million in 2023.

Peer Comparison

1. Industry-wide Analysis: The overall revenue growth trend in the insurance and financial services industry is affected by the economic environment and market demand. The overall market recovery in 2024 saw many companies reporting revenue growth, indicating an overall industry recovery.

2. Peer Evaluation Analysis: Bar Harbor's total operating revenue growth rate of 3.89% is at an average level in the industry. If other companies in the same industry have a revenue growth rate above 5%, it may indicate that bar harbor faces certain pressure in the market competition and needs to take measures to enhance its market share.

Summary

Bar Harbor's revenue growth in 2024 reflects improved market demand and increased sales of its core business. However, the decline in EBIT and cost control pressure suggest challenges in the company's operations. Overall, the company's financial situation shows potential for growth, but also requires attention to cost management and market competition.

Opportunities

1. With the global trade and economic recovery, Bar Harbor can capitalize on the opportunity to expand its market share, especially in the insurance business.

2. Technological innovation and digital transformation provide opportunities to enhance efficiency and service levels.

3. The overall industry recovery and increased consumer demand for insurance products may support the company's revenue growth.

Risks

1. Uncertainty in global economic recovery and high inflation and interest rates may put pressure on operating revenue.

2. Tight global supply of crude oil and natural gas may affect global commodity trade, which in turn may affect the company's business.

3. Increased external risks and rising trade protectionism may pose challenges to Bar Harbor's overseas operations and international trade services.

data.title: Daiwa is bullish on Hesai Group (HSAI.US), with a target price of US$35.
data.description:
Daiwa Securities, Japan's second-largest securities firm and investment bank, initiates coverage on hesai group (HSAI.US) with a "buy" rating and a target price of $35, indicating significant upside potential from the current share price. As of March 11, hesai Group's stock price soared 50.41% to close at $24.08, reflecting strong market confidence in its prospects.

The report notes that Hesai Group is the third-largest LiDAR manufacturer in China (by shipments), with a 26% market share in 2024. The company drives LiDAR product upgrades through its self-developed ASIC architecture and noise tolerance technology, ranking first in the number of global LiDAR patent applications. Daiwa Securities expects a CAGR of 78% from 2024 to 2027.

Daiwa Securities believes that as LiDAR chip technology continues to break through and shipments grow rapidly, Hesai Group's gross margin is expected to improve from 2024 to 2027, mainly due to the expected faster decline in production costs than the average selling price (ASP). The company has focused on chip-based LiDAR technology since 2017, enabling cost optimization potential similar to the "Moore's Law" in the semiconductor industry.

In addition, the company has secured multiple orders from mainstream carmakers in the ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) field, including Changan, Qirui, li auto, Xiaomi, Geely, and SAIC Volkswagen, and is expected to have a China ADAS LiDAR market share of over 30% in 2025-2027.

Daiwa Securities says that with its technological leadership and market share advantages, Hesai Group has long-term growth potential and is bullish on its future performance.

data.title: GECC's Q4 2024 Earnings: Navigating Growth Amidst Challenges
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! We're diving into the latest earnings call from great elm capital Corp (GECC), and let me tell you, it's a rollercoaster ride of growth, challenges, and strategic maneuvers. gecc just released its Q4 and full-year 2024 financial results, and there's a lot to unpack. So, let's get started!



THE GOOD, THE BAD, AND THE UGLY

First, the good news: GECC had a strong 2024, raising nearly $150 million in capital through multiple equity and note issuances. They grew their portfolio substantially and enhanced their cash-generating ability with the formation of their distinctive CLO JV. But here's the kicker: despite a 48.7% quarter-over-quarter decline in net investment income (NII) to $2.1 million, GECC increased its quarterly dividend by 5.7% to $0.37 per share for Q1 2025. That's confidence, folks!

Now, the bad news: that 48.7% drop in NII is a significant hit. It was primarily due to the uneven cadence of cash flows from the CLO JV, which is expected given the early stage of the underlying CLO investments. But GECC isn't sitting idly by. They've raised $13.2 million of equity at Net Asset Value from Summit Grove Partners, supported by a $3.3 million investment by Great Elm Group, Inc. in SGP. This capital raise strengthens their position and provides additional funds for portfolio growth.

And the ugly? The company's NAV per share decreased due to dividends exceeding NII in the period. But don't worry, GECC has a plan. They're deploying additional capital into investments with attractive risk-adjusted returns and expect to deliver strong NII in 2025 as CLO distributions materialize.

STRATEGIES FOR STABILITY

So, how does GECC plan to stabilize its income streams? Here are the key strategies:

1. Capital Raising: GECC successfully raised $13.2 million of equity at Net Asset Value from Summit Grove Partners in December 2024. This capital raise strengthens their position and provides additional funds for portfolio growth.

2. Dividend Strategy: Despite the decline in NII, GECC increased its quarterly dividend by 5.7% to $0.37 per share for Q1 2025. This forward-looking stance suggests management's confidence in future earnings potential and their expectation that CLO JV distributions will stabilize at higher levels or that the raised capital will be deployed into high-yielding opportunities.

3. Portfolio Management: GECC's portfolio construction shows disciplined yield hunting, capturing a 300 basis point spread between new investments (12.9% yield) and exits (9.9% yield). The 72% floating-rate exposure provides both inflation protection and stability in the current interest rate environment.

4. Asset Coverage Ratio: The improved asset coverage ratio of 169.7% enhances downside protection for debtholders while maintaining sufficient investment flexibility. This ratio reflects enhanced balance sheet stability.

5. Future Expectations: Management expects to deliver strong NII in 2025 as CLO distributions materialize and additional capital is deployed into investments with attractive risk-adjusted returns.

THE RISKS AND REWARDS

Now, let's talk about the risks and rewards of relying on the CLO JV for future growth. The potential benefits include enhanced cash generation and high returns over time. The CLO JV's weighted average current yield on the debt portfolio is 12.4%, with 72% exposure to floating-rate instruments, which provides both inflation protection and stability in the current interest rate environment. Additionally, the improved asset coverage ratio of 169.7% enhances downside protection for debtholders while maintaining sufficient investment flexibility.

However, there are potential risks associated with relying on the CLO JV for future growth. The uneven cash flows from the CLO JV can impact NII in the short term, as seen in the fourth quarter of 2024. Additionally, the company's earnings were affected by the lumpiness of cash distributions from CLOs, which are uneven at the beginning of their life. There is also a potential risk associated with the company's investment in Maverick Gaming, which is marked at a distressed level. Furthermore, the company's NAV per share decreased due to dividends exceeding NII in the period, which could impact its ability to maintain its dividend payout in the future.

DIVIDEND SUSTAINABILITY AND GROWTH

The 5.7% increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.37 per share for the first quarter of 2025 reflects GECC's confidence in its future earnings potential. This increase is significant because it comes despite a 48.7% decrease in net investment income (NII) in the fourth quarter of 2024, which was attributed to the uneven cadence of cash flows from the CLO JV. The management's decision to increase the dividend suggests that they anticipate either a stabilization of CLO JV distributions at higher levels or successful deployment of raised capital into high-yielding opportunities. This forward-looking stance indicates that GECC expects to deliver strong NII in 2025 as CLO distributions materialize and additional capital is deployed into investments with attractive risk-adjusted returns.

For shareholders, the implications of this dividend increase are twofold. First, it signals a commitment to returning value to shareholders, as evidenced by the 13.7% annualized yield based on the recent share price. Second, it suggests that the company is confident in its ability to sustain and potentially grow dividends in the future. However, shareholders should also be aware of the risks associated with the company's investment strategy, particularly the lumpiness of cash distributions from CLOs, which can impact earnings and dividend sustainability. The company's management has acknowledged this risk and has plans to mitigate it by growing the CLO JV and diversifying its investment portfolio over time.



THE BOTTOM LINE

So, what's the bottom line? GECC is navigating growth amidst challenges, and they're doing it with confidence and strategic maneuvers. The 48.7% quarter-over-quarter decline in NII is a significant hit, but GECC is mitigating this volatility with capital raising, dividend strategy, portfolio management, and future expectations. The risks and rewards of relying on the CLO JV for future growth are clear, and the dividend sustainability and growth implications for shareholders are significant. But one thing is for sure: GECC is a company on the move, and they're not afraid to take calculated risks to achieve their goals. So, stay tuned, folks, because this is one story you won't want to miss!

data.title: Bitcoin Dominance Hits New High as Altcoins Falter
data.description:
Matrixport, a prominent digital asset financial services platform, has released a report highlighting a significant surge in Bitcoin's dominance within the cryptocurrency market. According to the report, Bitcoin's dominance has reached a new high in this cycle, surpassing its previous peak. This development indicates that the recent rally in altcoins was short-lived, lasting only about a month from November to early December of last year.

The altcoin rally began in November following the election of Donald Trump, but it was abruptly halted in early December. The shift in market focus was driven by an unexpectedly strong US employment report, which led to a more hawkish stance from the Federal Reserve. This increased downside pressure on risk assets, including altcoins, causing traders to rotate back to Bitcoin despite some pullback in its price.

Seasoned traders have returned to Bitcoin, as its performance remains significantly better than the overall market. The current market sentiment is hopeful for improving liquidity to further drive Bitcoin's rise. However, with the Federal Reserve maintaining a hawkish tone, the next rally may require more patience and strategic planning.

This shift in market dynamics underscores the resilience of Bitcoin as a dominant force in the cryptocurrency landscape. Despite the volatility and short-lived rallies of altcoins, Bitcoin continues to be the preferred choice for many investors, especially during times of economic uncertainty. The report from Matrixport provides valuable insights into the current state of the cryptocurrency market and the factors influencing investor behavior.
